description: The `personalities` array emitted into `web/data/projects.json` (and synced to docs/data/projects.json) alongside the existing `agents` block. Drives the About-page Personality Registry modal (Story 6 / FR-022..FR-024). Order matches the rotation order (slug-sorted) for stable display.

slug.description: Filename stem of the personality prompt file under agents/prompts/personalities/. Used as the rotation pointer; stable across display-name changes.

display_name.description: Canonical display name WITHOUT the '(simulated)' suffix. The website prepends/appends the suffix at render time, never bakes it in.

summary.description: ≤ 14-word one-line summary shown in the registry modal row.

sources.description: Public-record source titles the persona prompt is grounded in. Rendered as a 'Grounded on' footer in the detail view.

prompt_repo_path.description: Repo-relative path to the prompt file.

prompt_raw_url.description: Raw GitHub URL — what the registry modal fetches to render the prompt body via the existing fetchAndRenderMarkdownInto helper (spec-007).

prompt_github_url.description: Browser-friendly GitHub URL — the 'View on GitHub' footer link in the detail view.

examples.summary: 19th-c. English mathematician; annotator of Babbage's Analytical Engine, early computing visionary.
sources: Notes by the Translator on Menabrea's Sketch of the Analytical Engine (1843)
MacTutor History of Mathematics, 'Augusta Ada King-Noel'
Stephen Wolfram, 'Untangling the Tale of Ada Lovelace' (2015)

examples.summary: Israeli-American psychologist, Princeton; Nobel laureate, heuristics and biases.
sources: Nobel Memorial Lecture: Maps of Bounded Rationality (2002)
Tversky & Kahneman, Judgment under Uncertainty: Heuristics and Biases, Science (1974)
